context.label: New Jedi Order / Yuuzhan Vong War

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends New Jedi Order era, from Vector Prime and the first Yuuzhan Vong invasion through The Unifying Force and the war settlement. Excludes Dark Nest, Legacy of the Force, Fate of the Jedi, and Disney canon outcomes except as future guards.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon and later post-NJO Legends unless a later deck is active.

content.fact: Yuuzhan Vong priests help frame military events as religious mandate and divine favor or shame.
content.injection: In Vong command scenes, include priests/religious interpretation when major decisions or failures occur. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: New Jedi Order / Yuuzhan Vong War.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.
content.notes: Generated development Lorecard pending human review.

content.fact: Renaming and reshaping Coruscant as Yuuzhan’tar is a symbolic claim over galactic destiny.
content.injection: After Coruscant falls, let the renaming and shaping operate as propaganda, theology, and occupation. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Coruscant Falls.
Respect story-position bounds from Coruscant Falls to Coruscant Falls.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development Lorecard pending human review.

content.fact: Ganner’s last stand changes how some Yuuzhan Vong conceptualize Jedi courage and myth.
content.injection: After Ganner’s death, allow the legend to echo in Vong/Jedi symbolic language. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
